violations.current_code: # Page additions file defines locators as direct properties: self.plan_pause_info = page.locator(...)
violations.expected_code: # Locators should be in Elements class and accessed via .elements. namespace
violations.explanation: The page_additions file defines locators as direct properties (self.plan_pause_info = page.locator(...)) instead of using the Elements container pattern. According to the framework pattern shown in the mapping report and existing page objects, locators should be defined in an Elements inner class and accessed via page_obj.elements.locator_name. This is a structural issue that affects all locators in the page_additions file.

fix_details.description: Restructure locators to use Elements inner class pattern
fix_details.required_structure:
class Elements:
    plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
    billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
    # ... other locators as string selectors

# Then in __init__:
# Access via page.locator(self.elements.plan_pause_info)

violations.explanation: Framework coding standards require ALL expect() assertions to have explicit timeout parameters. This is Checkpoint D14: 'All expect() calls have explicit timeout parameter'. Review shows all expect() calls in the script DO have timeout parameters, so this is actually PASSED. Removing this as a violation.

fix_details.note: All expect() calls have explicit timeouts - no fix needed

violations.expected_code:

violations.explanation: Using if/else pattern to check element count before verification. This is a soft verification pattern that Prohibition 2 explicitly forbids. The test case expected result states 'Verify the plan pause information is not displayed' - this is a required verification, not optional. The if statement makes it soft. Should use direct expect() or a page object method that uses expect() internally.

violations.expected_code:
# ADD TO Elements CLASS:

class Elements:
    # Billing Cycle Period Card locators
    plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
    billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
    complimentary_pages_progress_bar = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-progress-bar']"
    complimentary_pages_value = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-value']"
    complimentary_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-icon']"
    complimentary_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-message']"
    additional_pages_progress_bar = "[data-testid='additional-pages-progress-bar']"
    additional_pages_value = "[data-testid='additional-pages-value']"
    additional_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-icon']"
    additional_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-message']"
    complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"
    additional_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"
violations.explanation: The page_additions file defines locators as direct properties (self.locator_name = page.locator(...)) instead of using the Elements inner class pattern. According to Checkpoint F04 and the framework pattern shown in existing page objects like PrintHistoryPage in the mapping report, locators should be defined as string selectors in an Elements inner class, then accessed via page.locator(self.elements.locator_name) or as properties. The current pattern creates Locator objects directly which is not the framework standard. This affects all 10 locators defined in the file.

fix_details.description: Restructure all locators to use Elements inner class pattern with string selectors

fix_priority_order.fix_approach: Replace every page.locator('hardcoded selector') in the test function body with the corresponding page object property or method. For locators defined in print_history_page_additions.py, first ensure they are properly structured in an Elements class, then access via print_history_page.elements.{locator_name} or use the provided verification methods. For tooltips, add tooltip locators to page_additions and reference via page object.

Restructure the page_additions file to define all locators as string selectors in an Elements inner class following the framework pattern. Change from 'self.locator_name = page.locator(selector)' to 'class Elements: locator_name = selector'. This must be fixed before the test script can properly reference these locators.

Add actual verification assertions for steps that currently have only logging. Step 20 needs screenshot capture and visual comparison. Step 21 needs viewport resizing and responsive layout verification. Step 3 needs verification that event shift succeeded using GeminiRAHelper.verify_rails_admin_info().

Remove the if/else pattern that checks element count before verification. Replace with direct expect() assertion or use the page object method verify_plan_pause_info_not_displayed() which should handle the check internally with proper assertions.

Consider using PrintHistoryHelper methods for print history operations where available. The mapping report shows PrintHistoryHelper has multiple methods for billing cycle verification and page usage validation that could simplify the test script.
